Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class Order

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.Order
public class Order extends Object
Order

The message describing an order

    Optional<String>
    identifier()
    Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).
    Optional<String>
    identifierIssuingRegionCode()
    A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading.

    identifierType()
    The identifier type of the asset being ordered.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime>
    lastUpdateTime()
    Time of the last order update

    maxSellQuantity()
    The maximum number of shares to be sold if this is a notional SELL order of an Equity asset type.

    name()
    System generated name of the order.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<NotionalValue>
    notionalValue()
    Notional quantity of the order, measured in USD.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OrderDate>
    orderDate()
    The date on which the order will go to the market: must either be "today" or the next valid trading day.
    Optional<String>
    orderId()
    System generated unique id for the order.
    Optional<OrderRejectedReason>
    orderRejectedReason()
    When an order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    orderStatus()
    The processing status of the order
    Optional<OrderOrderType>
    orderType()
    The execution type of this order.
    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OrderPrevailingMarketPrice>
    prevailingMarketPrice()
    The prevailing market price, calculated as a weighted average of the fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places.

    timeInForce()
    Must be the value "DAY".

    • Order

extends CancelInitiator> cancelInitiator, Optional<String> cancelReason, Optional<? extends CancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientCancelReceivedTime, Optional<String> clientOrderId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderCommission> commission, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends CumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue, Optional<String> currencyCode, Optional<? extends List<TradingExecutions>> executions, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends ExtraReportingData> extraReportingData, Optional<? extends List<TradingFee>> fees, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<?     • Order

      public Order()

  • Method Details

    • accountId

      public Optional<String> accountId()
      The identifier of the account transacting this order

    • assetId

      public Optional<String> assetId()
      Apex Asset ID for this asset. This will not be returned in the initial CreateOrder response and will be available after an order completes validation. If the provided identifier does not match any Apex asset available for trading, an OrderRejectReason of "UNKNOWN_SECURITY" will be returned and the asset_id will not be set.

    • assetType

      public Optional<OrderAssetType> assetType()
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • averagePrices

      public Optional<List<TradingExecutedPrice>> averagePrices()
      The average prices, as weighted averages, across all executions in this order. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

      When asset_type = EQUITY or MUTUAL_FUND, there will be at most one value present, with a type of PRICE_PER_UNIT. This will have up to 4 decimal places for USD amounts less than $1, and a maximum of two for larger USD amounts.

      When asset_type = FIXED_INCOME, there may be more than one value present which would have a type other than PRICE_PER_UNIT. Price values in PERCENTAGE_OF_PAR will have up to 4 decimal places of precision, and price values measured in yields will support up to 5 decimal places.

    • brokerCapacity

      public Optional<OrderBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity()
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • cancelInitiator

      public Optional<CancelInitiator> cancelInitiator()
      Output only field that is required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. This field denotes the initiator of the cancel request. This field will be present when provided on the CancelOrderRequest

    • cancelReason

      public Optional<String> cancelReason()
      Used to explain why an order is canceled

    • cancelRejectedReason

      public Optional<CancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason()
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • clientCancelReceivedTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientCancelReceivedTime()
      Output only field for Equity Orders related to CAT reporting on behalf of clients. This field will be present when provided on the CancelOrderRequest

    • clientOrderId

      public Optional<String> clientOrderId()
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime()
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • commission

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OrderCommission> commission()
      A custom commission to be applied to this order. When specifying an AMOUNT type, the value represents a notional amount measured in the currency of the order.

    • createTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime()
      Time of the order creation

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<CumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue()
      The sum of order quantity & price, across all fills, reported in the currency specified in the order. (This will be rounded to 2 decimal places for USD currencies). Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • currencyCode

      public Optional<String> currencyCode()
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • executions

      public Optional<List<TradingExecutions>> executions()
      The execution-level details that compose this order

    • extraReportingData

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<ExtraReportingData> extraReportingData()
      Any reporting data provided by the SetExtraReportingData endpoint.

    • fees

      public Optional<List<TradingFee>> fees()
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • filledQuantity

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<FilledQuantity> filledQuantity()
      The summed quantity value across all fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • identifier

      public Optional<String> identifier()
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • identifierIssuingRegionCode

      public Optional<String> identifierIssuingRegionCode()
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • identifierType

      public Optional<OrderIdentifierType> identifierType()
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • lastUpdateTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> lastUpdateTime()
      Time of the last order update

    • letterOfIntent

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<LetterOfIntent> letterOfIntent()
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • limitPrice

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<LimitPrice> limitPrice()
      The limit price for this order.

    • maxSellQuantity

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<MaxSellQuantity> maxSellQuantity()
      The maximum number of shares to be sold if this is a notional SELL order of an Equity asset type. (Prohibited for other side or asset_type inputs.)

      This will only be recognized for clients configured not to use OMS checks. When specified, must be greater than 0 and can't exceed 5 decimal places.

    • name

      public Optional<String> name()
      System generated name of the order.

    • notionalValue

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<NotionalValue> notionalValue()
      Notional quantity of the order, measured in USD. Maximum 2 decimal place precision. For Equities: This represents the maximum amount to be spent. The final order may may have a smaller notional amount. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for BUY orders. The order will be transacted at the full notional amount specified. For Fixed Income: Not supported, you must specify a `quantity` value.

    • orderDate

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OrderDate> orderDate()
      The date on which the order will go to the market: must either be "today" or the next valid trading day. If the current day is not a valid trading day, then the next valid market day must be specified. If the current time is within 5 minutes prior to market close, the next valid market day may be specified. If the current time is after market close, and before midnight Eastern, then the next valid market day must be specified. In all other cases, the current day, Eastern must be specified.

    • orderId

      public Optional<String> orderId()
      System generated unique id for the order.

    • orderRejectedReason

      public Optional<OrderRejectedReason> orderRejectedReason()
      When an order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • orderStatus

      public Optional<OrderStatus> orderStatus()
      The processing status of the order

    • orderType

      public Optional<OrderOrderType> orderType()
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, and LIMIT are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • prevailingMarketPrice

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OrderPrevailingMarketPrice> prevailingMarketPrice()
      The prevailing market price, calculated as a weighted average of the fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

    • quantity

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OrderQuantity> quantity()
      Numeric quantity of the order. For Equities: Represents the number of shares, must be greater than zero and may not exceed 5 decimal places. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for SELL orders. Represents the number of shares, up to a maximum of 3 decimal places. For Fixed Income: Represents the par (face-value) amount being ordered, and may not exceed two decimal places for USD-based currencies. Either a quantity or notional_value MUST be specified (but not both).

    • rightsOfAccumulation

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<RightsOfAccumulation> rightsOfAccumulation()
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

    • side

      public Optional<OrderSide> side()
      The side of this order.

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public Optional<List<OrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions()
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • stopPrice

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<StopPrice> stopPrice()
      The stop price for this order. Only allowed for equities.

    • timeInForce

      public Optional<OrderTimeInForce> timeInForce()
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • tradingSession

      public Optional<OrderTradingSession> tradingSession()
      Which TradingSession to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • builder

      public static Order.Builder builder()

    • withAccountId

      public Order withAccountId(String accountId)
      The identifier of the account transacting this order

    • withAccountId

      public Order withAccountId(Optional<String> accountId)
      The identifier of the account transacting this order

    • withAssetId

      public Order withAssetId(String assetId)
      Apex Asset ID for this asset. This will not be returned in the initial CreateOrder response and will be available after an order completes validation. If the provided identifier does not match any Apex asset available for trading, an OrderRejectReason of "UNKNOWN_SECURITY" will be returned and the asset_id will not be set.

    • withAssetId

      public Order withAssetId(Optional<String> assetId)
      Apex Asset ID for this asset. This will not be returned in the initial CreateOrder response and will be available after an order completes validation. If the provided identifier does not match any Apex asset available for trading, an OrderRejectReason of "UNKNOWN_SECURITY" will be returned and the asset_id will not be set.

    • withAssetType

      public Order withAssetType(OrderAssetType assetType)
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • withAssetType

      public Order withAssetType(Optional<? extends OrderAssetType> assetType)
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • withAveragePrices

      public Order withAveragePrices(List<TradingExecutedPrice> averagePrices)
      The average prices, as weighted averages, across all executions in this order. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

      When asset_type = EQUITY or MUTUAL_FUND, there will be at most one value present, with a type of PRICE_PER_UNIT. This will have up to 4 decimal places for USD amounts less than $1, and a maximum of two for larger USD amounts.

      When asset_type = FIXED_INCOME, there may be more than one value present which would have a type other than PRICE_PER_UNIT. Price values in PERCENTAGE_OF_PAR will have up to 4 decimal places of precision, and price values measured in yields will support up to 5 decimal places.

    • withAveragePrices

      public Order withAveragePrices(Optional<? extends List<TradingExecutedPrice>> averagePrices)
      The average prices, as weighted averages, across all executions in this order. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

      When asset_type = EQUITY or MUTUAL_FUND, there will be at most one value present, with a type of PRICE_PER_UNIT. This will have up to 4 decimal places for USD amounts less than $1, and a maximum of two for larger USD amounts.

      When asset_type = FIXED_INCOME, there may be more than one value present which would have a type other than PRICE_PER_UNIT. Price values in PERCENTAGE_OF_PAR will have up to 4 decimal places of precision, and price values measured in yields will support up to 5 decimal places.

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public Order withBrokerCapacity(OrderBrokerCapacity brokerCapacity)
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public Order withBrokerCapacity(Optional<? extends OrderBrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity)
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • withCancelInitiator

      public Order withCancelInitiator(CancelInitiator cancelInitiator)
      Output only field that is required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. This field denotes the initiator of the cancel request. This field will be present when provided on the CancelOrderRequest

    • withCancelInitiator

      public Order withCancelInitiator(Optional<? extends CancelInitiator> cancelInitiator)
      Output only field that is required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. This field denotes the initiator of the cancel request. This field will be present when provided on the CancelOrderRequest

    • withCancelReason

      public Order withCancelReason(String cancelReason)
      Used to explain why an order is canceled

    • withCancelReason

      public Order withCancelReason(Optional<String> cancelReason)
      Used to explain why an order is canceled

    • withCancelRejectedReason

      public Order withCancelRejectedReason(CancelRejectedReason cancelRejectedReason)
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • withCancelRejectedReason

      public Order withCancelRejectedReason(Optional<? extends CancelRejectedReason> cancelRejectedReason)
      Used to denote when a cancel request has been rejected.

    • withClientCancelReceivedTime

      public Order withClientCancelReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientCancelReceivedTime)
      Output only field for Equity Orders related to CAT reporting on behalf of clients. This field will be present when provided on the CancelOrderRequest

    • withClientCancelReceivedTime

      public Order withClientCancelReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientCancelReceivedTime)
      Output only field for Equity Orders related to CAT reporting on behalf of clients. This field will be present when provided on the CancelOrderRequest

    • withClientOrderId

      public Order withClientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • withClientOrderId

      public Order withClientOrderId(Optional<String> clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public Order withClientReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientReceivedTime)
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public Order withClientReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime)
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • withCommission

      public Order withCommission(OrderCommission commission)
      A custom commission to be applied to this order. When specifying an AMOUNT type, the value represents a notional amount measured in the currency of the order.

    • withCommission

      public Order withCommission(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderCommission> commission)
      A custom commission to be applied to this order. When specifying an AMOUNT type, the value represents a notional amount measured in the currency of the order.

    • withCreateTime

      public Order withCreateTime(OffsetDateTime createTime)
      Time of the order creation

    • withCreateTime

      public Order withCreateTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime)
      Time of the order creation

    • withCumulativeNotionalValue

      public Order withCumulativeNotionalValue(CumulativeNotionalValue cumulativeNotionalValue)
      The sum of order quantity & price, across all fills, reported in the currency specified in the order. (This will be rounded to 2 decimal places for USD currencies). Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • withCumulativeNotionalValue

      public Order withCumulativeNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends CumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue)
      The sum of order quantity & price, across all fills, reported in the currency specified in the order. (This will be rounded to 2 decimal places for USD currencies). Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public Order withCurrencyCode(String currencyCode)
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withCurrencyCode

      public Order withCurrencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withExecutions

      public Order withExecutions(List<TradingExecutions> executions)
      The execution-level details that compose this order

    • withExecutions

      public Order withExecutions(Optional<? extends List<TradingExecutions>> executions)
      The execution-level details that compose this order

    • withExtraReportingData

      public Order withExtraReportingData(ExtraReportingData extraReportingData)
      Any reporting data provided by the SetExtraReportingData endpoint.

    • withExtraReportingData

      public Order withExtraReportingData(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends ExtraReportingData> extraReportingData)
      Any reporting data provided by the SetExtraReportingData endpoint.

    • withFees

      public Order withFees(List<TradingFee> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • withFees

      public Order withFees(Optional<? extends List<TradingFee>> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • withFilledQuantity

      public Order withFilledQuantity(FilledQuantity filledQuantity)
      The summed quantity value across all fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • withFilledQuantity

      public Order withFilledQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends FilledQuantity> filledQuantity)
      The summed quantity value across all fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no fill information.

    • withIdentifier

      public Order withIdentifier(String identifier)
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • withIdentifier

      public Order withIdentifier(Optional<String> identifier)
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode

      public Order withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode(String identifierIssuingRegionCode)
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode

      public Order withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode(Optional<String> identifierIssuingRegionCode)
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • withIdentifierType

      public Order withIdentifierType(OrderIdentifierType identifierType)
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • withIdentifierType

      public Order withIdentifierType(Optional<? extends OrderIdentifierType> identifierType)
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • withLastUpdateTime

      public Order withLastUpdateTime(OffsetDateTime lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last order update

    • withLastUpdateTime

      public Order withLastUpdateTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last order update

    • withLetterOfIntent

      public Order withLetterOfIntent(LetterOfIntent letterOfIntent)
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • withLetterOfIntent

      public Order withLetterOfIntent(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends LetterOfIntent> letterOfIntent)
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • withLimitPrice

      public Order withLimitPrice(LimitPrice limitPrice)
      The limit price for this order.

    • withLimitPrice

      public Order withLimitPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends LimitPrice> limitPrice)
      The limit price for this order.

    • withMaxSellQuantity

      public Order withMaxSellQuantity(MaxSellQuantity maxSellQuantity)
      The maximum number of shares to be sold if this is a notional SELL order of an Equity asset type. (Prohibited for other side or asset_type inputs.)

      This will only be recognized for clients configured not to use OMS checks. When specified, must be greater than 0 and can't exceed 5 decimal places.

    • withMaxSellQuantity

      public Order withMaxSellQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MaxSellQuantity> maxSellQuantity)
      The maximum number of shares to be sold if this is a notional SELL order of an Equity asset type. (Prohibited for other side or asset_type inputs.)

      This will only be recognized for clients configured not to use OMS checks. When specified, must be greater than 0 and can't exceed 5 decimal places.

    • withName

      public Order withName(String name)
      System generated name of the order.

    • withName

      public Order withName(Optional<String> name)
      System generated name of the order.

    • withNotionalValue

      public Order withNotionalValue(NotionalValue notionalValue)
      Notional quantity of the order, measured in USD. Maximum 2 decimal place precision. For Equities: This represents the maximum amount to be spent. The final order may may have a smaller notional amount. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for BUY orders. The order will be transacted at the full notional amount specified. For Fixed Income: Not supported, you must specify a `quantity` value.

    • withNotionalValue

      public Order withNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends NotionalValue> notionalValue)
      Notional quantity of the order, measured in USD. Maximum 2 decimal place precision. For Equities: This represents the maximum amount to be spent. The final order may may have a smaller notional amount. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for BUY orders. The order will be transacted at the full notional amount specified. For Fixed Income: Not supported, you must specify a `quantity` value.

    • withOrderDate

      public Order withOrderDate(OrderDate orderDate)
      The date on which the order will go to the market: must either be "today" or the next valid trading day. If the current day is not a valid trading day, then the next valid market day must be specified. If the current time is within 5 minutes prior to market close, the next valid market day may be specified. If the current time is after market close, and before midnight Eastern, then the next valid market day must be specified. In all other cases, the current day, Eastern must be specified.

    • withOrderDate

      public Order withOrderDate(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderDate> orderDate)
      The date on which the order will go to the market: must either be "today" or the next valid trading day. If the current day is not a valid trading day, then the next valid market day must be specified. If the current time is within 5 minutes prior to market close, the next valid market day may be specified. If the current time is after market close, and before midnight Eastern, then the next valid market day must be specified. In all other cases, the current day, Eastern must be specified.

    • withOrderId

      public Order withOrderId(String orderId)
      System generated unique id for the order.

    • withOrderId

      public Order withOrderId(Optional<String> orderId)
      System generated unique id for the order.

    • withOrderRejectedReason

      public Order withOrderRejectedReason(OrderRejectedReason orderRejectedReason)
      When an order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • withOrderRejectedReason

      public Order withOrderRejectedReason(Optional<? extends OrderRejectedReason> orderRejectedReason)
      When an order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • withOrderStatus

      public Order withOrderStatus(OrderStatus orderStatus)
      The processing status of the order

    • withOrderStatus

      public Order withOrderStatus(Optional<? extends OrderStatus> orderStatus)
      The processing status of the order

    • withOrderType

      public Order withOrderType(OrderOrderType orderType)
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, and LIMIT are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • withOrderType

      public Order withOrderType(Optional<? extends OrderOrderType> orderType)
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, and LIMIT are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • withPrevailingMarketPrice

      public Order withPrevailingMarketPrice(OrderPrevailingMarketPrice prevailingMarketPrice)
      The prevailing market price, calculated as a weighted average of the fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

    • withPrevailingMarketPrice

      public Order withPrevailingMarketPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderPrevailingMarketPrice> prevailingMarketPrice)
      The prevailing market price, calculated as a weighted average of the fills in this order, up to a maximum of 5 decimal places. Will be absent if an order has no executions.

    • withQuantity

      public Order withQuantity(OrderQuantity quantity)
      Numeric quantity of the order. For Equities: Represents the number of shares, must be greater than zero and may not exceed 5 decimal places. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for SELL orders. Represents the number of shares, up to a maximum of 3 decimal places. For Fixed Income: Represents the par (face-value) amount being ordered, and may not exceed two decimal places for USD-based currencies. Either a quantity or notional_value MUST be specified (but not both).

    • withQuantity

      public Order withQuantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OrderQuantity> quantity)
      Numeric quantity of the order. For Equities: Represents the number of shares, must be greater than zero and may not exceed 5 decimal places. For Mutual Funds: Only supported for SELL orders. Represents the number of shares, up to a maximum of 3 decimal places. For Fixed Income: Represents the par (face-value) amount being ordered, and may not exceed two decimal places for USD-based currencies. Either a quantity or notional_value MUST be specified (but not both).

    • withRightsOfAccumulation

      public Order withRightsOfAccumulation(RightsOfAccumulation rightsOfAccumulation)
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

    • withRightsOfAccumulation

      public Order withRightsOfAccumulation(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends RightsOfAccumulation> rightsOfAccumulation)
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

    • withSide

      public Order withSide(OrderSide side)
      The side of this order.

    • withSide

      public Order withSide(Optional<? extends OrderSide> side)
      The side of this order.

    • withSpecialReportingInstructions

      public Order withSpecialReportingInstructions(List<OrderSpecialReportingInstructions> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • withSpecialReportingInstructions

      public Order withSpecialReportingInstructions(Optional<? extends List<OrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • withStopPrice

      public Order withStopPrice(StopPrice stopPrice)
      The stop price for this order. Only allowed for equities.

    • withStopPrice

      public Order withStopPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StopPrice> stopPrice)
      The stop price for this order. Only allowed for equities.

    • withTimeInForce

      public Order withTimeInForce(OrderTimeInForce timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • withTimeInForce

      public Order withTimeInForce(Optional<? extends OrderTimeInForce> timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • withTradingSession

      public Order withTradingSession(OrderTradingSession tradingSession)
      Which TradingSession to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • withTradingSession

      public Order withTradingSession(Optional<? extends OrderTradingSession> tradingSession)
      Which TradingSession to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
